author: obsidian-ttrpg-community
description: This plugin has not been manually reviewed by Obsidian staff. Visualise relationships between notes — for worldbuilding, fiction, TTRPG campaigns, genealogies, or any project where seeing how things connect matters.

manifest.description: Visualise relationships between notes — for worldbuilding, fiction, TTRPG campaigns, genealogies, or any project where seeing how things connect matters.

name: Relations

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/Obsidian-TTRPG-Community/Relations/compare/0.16.1...0.18.0

feat: node display customization - ring color and node badges

0.17.0 - Ring color
- New "Ring color" settings section: pick a frontmatter property and define
  exact-match value-to-color rules. Notes whose property matches a rule
  render with a colored outer ring.
- Settings UI lets users add/remove rules with a color picker per rule.
- Cache invalidation hooked up so settings changes rebuild affected nodes.
- Ring color renders on every view mode (basic graph, family-graph,
  family-tree) including the focus note itself.
- Pure helper resolveRingColor() factored out for testing.
- Settings panel preserves scroll position when adding/removing rules
  (previously jumped to top on every change).

0.18.0 - Node badges (corner icons + italic subtext)
- New "Node badges" settings section: three optional frontmatter properties
  drive a top-left icon, a top-right icon, and italic subtext beneath the
  node. Whatever the user puts in the property renders as-is, so emoji is
  the obvious primary use but short text also works.
- DOM overlay layer (src/node-badges.ts) sits above the Cytoscape canvas
  and pins badges to nodes. Listens to pan, zoom, resize, position, add,
  remove, data, and layoutstop events with rAF-coalesced redraw.
- Badges anchor to the ring's 45-degree diagonal (touching the circle's
  visible edge, not the bounding-box corner) for a clean attached look.
- Subtext sits below the node label.
- Everything scales 1:1 with Cytoscape's zoom via transform: scale() with
  corner-specific transform-origin, so badges remain proportional to the
  portrait at every zoom level.
- Respects the global "Show node labels" toggle - turning labels off
  also disables badges, keeping minimal-portraits mode minimal.
- Generic helper resolveFrontmatterString() factored out for testing.

Tests: 134 passing (105 before this release + 15 ring-color + 14
frontmatter-string).

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/Obsidian-TTRPG-Community/Relations/compare/0.15.4...0.16.1

feat: affair-child layout, soft-adjacency for lovers, scope: connected

Layout improvements for family-tree mode:

- Affair-child rule: when a child has two parents, one with a spouse-
  elsewhere and the other unmarried, the spouse-having parent is dropped
  from the layout. The child renders cleanly under the lover-parent.
  Biological data unchanged in the graph; only layout planning sees the
  simplified parent set. Helper computeEffectiveParents in
  src/family-parenting.ts, consumed by family-tree.ts and family-connectors.ts.

- Soft-adjacency (step 9c in family-tree.ts): lover-only single-parent
  units are pulled adjacent to their bloodline anchor in the partner row,
  clearing the anchor's full canonical subtree to avoid collisions with
  the anchor's own children. Multiple lovers stack outward in declaration
  order. The lover's full subtree (parent + descendants) is shifted
  together so children stay directly beneath their mother.

- Generation propagation now uses raw declared parents (both), while
  unit grouping uses the affair-filtered set. This fixes a regression
  where an affair child who later married was placed one row above
  their bloodline parent.

New code-block option scope: connected -- third scope alongside local
and full. Walks every edge reachable from the focus note (undirected,
any edge type, no depth limit). Shows the connected component containing
the focus, excluding disconnected islands.

Tests: 105 passing (+16 since 0.15.4).

releases.author: ObsidianTTRPGProject
releases.description:
Relations — recent changes
v0.12.2 — Auto-add block id on lock
Clicking lock on a code block without an id now generates one (e.g. rel-7f3a9c2b) and writes it into the block automatically — no more manual setup. Includes a fence guard so it never edits the wrong line, and callout/blockquote support so > -prefixed blocks stay valid.
v0.12.1 — Editable locked layouts
The lock button is now two actions: a save button that re-captures node positions in place after you drag them (no reset, no flicker), and a separate reset button that clears the layout and returns to auto. You can keep nudging a locked graph and save updates incrementally.
v0.12.0 — Lock layouts in place
New per-block layout locking: drag nodes where you want them, hit lock, and the positions persist across note refreshes and Obsidian restarts. Positions are keyed by the block's id and stored in plugin data; while locked the auto-layout is skipped so nothing reshuffles.
releases.name: 0.12.2 - Added ability to lock layouts in place.

releases.author: ObsidianTTRPGProject
releases.description:
Add optional node labels toggle

Hide the note name under each node for a portrait-only graph.
Controllable globally (Show node labels setting), live (Labels
toolbar button), or per-embed (labels: true/false in a code block).

- Labels-off zeroes the label, text background, and border so no
  pill or reserved space remains
- Layout skips label-width measurement when labels are hidden,
  packing nodes tighter
- Cosmetic setting — excluded from the graph cache hash, so
  toggling re-renders without a vault rescan
releases.name: 0.10.0 - Added ability to hide node labels.

releases.author: ObsidianTTRPGProject
releases.description:
Auto-review fixes for community-plugin submission

- manifest.json: authorUrl now points to the org, not the repo
- styles.css: expanded 3-digit hex colours to 6-digit
- package.json: build script now typechecks before bundling
- Added .github/workflows/release.yml for automated builds with
  artifact provenance attestation

No functional changes.

releases.author: ObsidianTTRPGProject
releases.description:
Remove family-tree mode, promote family-graph to canonical family view

The classical orthogonal-pipework chart never reached shippable quality
— multi-marriage layouts, marriage-bar clipping, and sibling-bar
staggering each had edge cases that broke in real vaults. The
graph-style family-graph view shares the same generation-aligned
positioning algorithm and edge-type differentiation but renders with
Cytoscape's native edges, avoiding the geometry edge cases entirely.

BREAKING:
- `family-tree: true` codeblock option no longer activates a special view
- `Family tree` side-panel toggle button removed
- `familyTree` plugin setting silently ignored on load

Migration: replace `family-tree: true` with `family-graph: true`.

Internals:
- applyFamilyTreeLayout → applyGenerationLayout, returns void
- Removed setupConnectorOverlay and ConnectorGeometry (~315 lines)
- Bundle ~5KB smaller
releases.name: 0.9.0 - Family-Graph Improvements
